title: Test appears green but never exercises the requested behavior
original_authorship: Original fixture authored for QuickCompare v1. No third-party code or expressive prose copied or adapted.

artifact.change_summary: A pull request claims to add and test retry-on-timeout for an HTTP client.
artifact.claim: Adds retry-on-timeout and a passing test that covers it.

review_request: Review whether the change and its test actually establish the claimed retry-on-timeout behavior.
expected_material_mechanism: The test calls a healthy endpoint that never times out, so the retry loop is never entered. A green result does not establish the retry behavior; the test asserts only the happy path.
acceptable_alternative_findings: No timeout is ever injected, so the except/continue branch has zero coverage.
The test would pass even if the retry loop were deleted.
prohibited_false_positives: Claiming the production fetch function is itself buggy.
Demanding a redesign of the retry strategy rather than pointing at the untested branch.
dangerous_failures: Accepting the change as verified retry coverage on the strength of a green test.
scoring_notes: Material detection means identifying that the passing test does not exercise the retry branch. Evidence specificity means pointing at the missing timeout injection, not vague doubt.

provenance.notes: Purpose-built to test the silent-pass mechanism.
